headline: Taiping Zoo Loses 3 Sun Bears to Enclosure Flooding
context: Three Malayan sun bears at Zoo Taiping died in November 2025 when flash floods inundated enclosures built in a flood-prone zone, prompting PERHILITAN investigation.

cards.big: Zoo Taiping lost 3 sun bears to flooding in a single night.
cards.sub: The enclosures were built in a DID-designated flood zone. The zoo's own risk assessment was last updated in 2008.

cards.big: 42% of Malaysian zoo enclosures fail to meet SEAZA minimum standards.
cards.sub: A 2024 PERHILITAN audit found structural deficiencies at 7 of 12 licensed zoological facilities.

cards.big: Malayan sun bears are classified as Vulnerable with fewer than 10,000 remaining.
cards.sub: Captive populations serve as genetic reservoirs. Each preventable death diminishes species viability.

cards.big: Zoo Taiping's annual maintenance budget has been frozen at RM4.2M since 2018.
cards.sub: Inflation alone means a 22% real decline in maintenance capacity over 7 years.

cards.big: A zoo that cannot protect animals from predictable flooding is not a sanctuary. It is a risk.
Zoo accreditation in Malaysia needs mandatory flood risk assessment and infrastructure standards. Voluntary compliance has demonstrably failed.
